channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Israel expands its bombardment in Lebanon as thousands flee widening war
item.description: MASNAA BORDER CROSSING, Lebanon (AP) — Israel expanded its bombardment in Lebanon on Saturday, hitting Beirut’s southern suburbs with a dozen airstrikes and striking a Palestinian refugee camp deep in the north for the first time as it targeted both Hezbollah and Hamas fighters.
item.dc:creator: BASSAM HATOUM, MELANIE LIDMAN and BASSEM MROUE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Former CIA head signals full-scale war inevitable if Israel 'goes all out'
item.description: Former Secretary of Defense and CIA Director Leon Panetta said the war in the Middle East is at a crucial point now and future actions will signal whether there will be an imminent “full scale war.
item.dc:creator: Joanne Haner, The Hill

item.title: Rising from the ashes: Israelis in nation's war-torn south move home a year after October 7 massacre
item.description: The unique communal structure of the kibbutz plays a vital role in fostering resilience and support as people heal from the devastating impact of the October 7 massacre with many now returning home.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Militants kill 6 Pakistani soldiers in a shootout
item.description: PESHAWAR, Pakistan (AP) — Militants killed six Pakistani soldiers in a shootout, the army said Saturday, the latest unrest in Khyber Pakhtunkhwa province bordering Afghanistan where armed groups including the Pakistani Taliban are active.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Most Americans say Israel-Hamas war will lead to wider war: Survey
item.description: Most Americans said the Israel-Hamas war will lead to wider conflict in the region involving other countries, according to a survey released on Friday.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: Congo finally begins mpox vaccinations in a drive to slow outbreaks
item.description: Congolese authorities have started vaccinations against mpox, nearly two months after the disease outbreak that spread from Congo to several other African countries and beyond was declared a global emergency.
item.dc:creator: Ruth Alonga, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Ukraine downs a Russian warplane and Russia claims gains in the east
item.description: Ukrainian forces say they have shot down a Russian fighter plane while Russia claimed it made gains in Ukraine’s east.
item.dc:creator: Samya Kullab And Elise Morton, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Thousands join pro-Palestinian rallies around the globe as Oct. 7 anniversary nears
item.description: Police used tear gas and water cannons to disperse violent demonstrators in Rome as tens of thousands pro-Palestinian protesters took to the streets in European cities and around the globe, calling for a cease-fire as the first anniversary of the Hamas attacks on Israel approached.
item.dc:creator: Giada Zampano And Sylvia Hui, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: FBI, DHS warn of possible violence on Oct. 7 anniversary
item.description: The statement said synagogues and mosques “present attractive targets for violent attacks or for hoax threats by a variety of threat actors.
item.dc:creator: Rich Johnson, The Hill

item.title: Rescue teams search for missing after floods, landslides kill at least 16 in Bosnia
item.description: Rescue teams on Saturday were searching for those still missing after flash floods and landslides hit parts of Bosnia, killing at least 16 people and injuring scores more.
item.dc:creator: Eldar Emric, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Iran's supreme leader makes rare speech after attacks on Israel
item.description: Iran's leader defended his country’s missile strikes on Israel this week, calling them “correct, logical, and lawful” in a rare public speech in Tehran on Friday.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, The Hill

item.title: Relatives say a whole family was killed in Israel's deadliest West Bank strike since Oct. 7
item.description: TULKAREM, West Bank (AP) — An Israeli airstrike on a West Bank cafe that the military said targeted Palestinian militants also killed a family of four, including two young children, relatives told The Associated Press on Friday.
item.dc:creator: NASSER NASSER and AREF TUFAHA, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: New MH370 project searches for answers on vanished plane
item.description: A new project is searching for answers about what happened to Malaysian Airlines Flight MH370 and its 239 passengers.
item.dc:creator: Jeff Arnold, NewsNation

item.title: New NATO chief Mark Rutte visits Ukraine in his first trip since taking office
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — New NATO Secretary-General Mark Rutte visited Ukraine on Thursday in his first official trip since taking office and pledging the alliance's continued support for Kyiv in its war with Russia.
item.dc:creator: HANNA ARHIROVA and SAMYA KULLAB, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Iran warns of 'decisive response' if Israel crosses 'red lines'
item.description: Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian warned this week at the Asia Cooperation Dialogue summit that Israel cannot act with "impunity" and "crossing [Iran's] red lines" will be met with a "decisive response.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Shooting near luxury Mexico resort leaves 1 dead, suspects flee on jet skis
item.description: A Mexican national was shot and killed Wednesday near a ritzy resort in Cancun before two suspects fled on jet skis, according to a local report.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Middle East latest: Israeli strike in Beirut killed 7 health and rescue workers
item.description: An Israeli strike overnight in the Lebanese capital Beirut killed seven health and rescue workers, an Islamic health organization said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: WATCH: US destroyer fires ballistic missile interceptors to defend Israel against Iranian barrage
item.description: The USS Cole and USS Bulkeley fired about a dozen ballistic missile interceptors to defend Israel from a barrage of nearly 200 Iranian missiles on Tuesday, officials said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 8 Israeli soldiers killed in Lebanon as Netanyahu says IDF engaged in 'tough war' with Hezbollah
item.description: Israel's military announced Wednesday that eight soldiers have died during "combat in southern Lebanon" as troops are wiping out Hezbollah infrastructure.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Mexico's army seizes local police weapons in cartel heartland amid gunfights, violence
item.description: The Mexican army has seized the firearms of local police in the Sinaloa city of Culiacan as cartel violence has escalated, dozens have been killed in the streets and distrust in the police grows.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: A US bomb from World War II explodes at a Japanese airport, causing a large crater in a taxiway
item.description: Officials said an investigation by the Self-Defense Forces and police confirmed that the explosion was caused by a 500-pound U.S. bomb and there was no further danger.
item.dc:creator: MARI YAMAGUCHI, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Claudia Sheinbaum sworn in as 1st female president of Mexico
item.description: MEXICO CITY (AP) — Claudia Sheinbaum was sworn in Tuesday as Mexico’s first female president, riding the enthusiasm over her predecessor’s social programs but also facing challenges that include stubbornly high levels of violence.
item.dc:creator: FABIOLA SÁNCHEZ and MARIANA MARTÍNEZ BARBA, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: US readiness plays 'significant' role in fending off Iranian attack on Israel
item.description: The U.S. once again assured Israel it stands ready to defend it against Iran just hours prior to Tehran launching a second large scale strike on Israel in which some 200 missiles were fired at it.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Iran fires at least 180 missiles into Israel as regionwide conflict grows
item.description: Iran fired dozens of missiles into Israel on Tuesday, prompting Israel to order residents to remain close to bomb shelters, while the U.S. warned of severe consequences if Iran launched a ballistic missile against Israel.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Iran says it fired dozens of missiles into Israel, where residents are warned to shelter in place
item.description: Israel says Iran has fired missiles at Israel. Israel warned Lebanese people earlier Tuesday to evacuate border communities to avoid harm from limited ground operations it said were launched against Hezbollah.
item.dc:creator: AAMER MADHANI, JULIA FRANKEL and BASSEM MROUE, WFLA, NBC

item.title: What to know about fighting in Lebanon and Gaza
item.description: Relentless Israeli airstrikes pounded Beirut’s southern suburbs overnight and closed off the main highway linking Lebanon with Syria, forcing civilians to flee across the border by foot.
item.dc:creator: Julia Frankel, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: A likely Russian artillery strike kills at least 6 at a Ukrainian market
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — An apparent Russian artillery strike hit a market in the southern Ukraine city of Kherson on Tuesday, killing at least six people and wounding three others, authorities said, on a morning when Ukrainians across the country were observing a minute’s silence for their military and
item.dc:creator: HANNA ARHIROVA and ILLIA NOVIKOV, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: South Korea unveils its most powerful missile, which could reach North Korea's underground bunkers
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — South Korea unveiled its most powerful ballistic missile and other weapons that could target North Korea during a massive Armed Forces Day ceremony on Tuesday, as the South's president warned the North's regime would collapse if it attempts to use nuclear weapons.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Israeli raids against Hezbollah signal a wider ground incursion into Lebanon could come soon
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — The Israeli military launched small ground raids against Hezbollah and sealed off communities along its northern border on Monday as Israeli artillery pounded southern Lebanon and signals grew that more forces could soon be sent across the border to fight the Iran-backed militants.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW LEE, SAMY MAGDY, ABBY SEWELL and NATALIE MELZER Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Hezbollah's acting leader vows to fight on after Nasrallah's death
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — Hezbollah's acting leader vowed Monday to continue battling Israel and said the militant group was prepared for a long fight even after much of its top command was wiped out, including its leader, Hassan Nasrallah.
item.dc:creator: ABBY SEWELL and NATALIE MELZER, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Who were the 7 high-ranking Hezbollah officials killed over the past week?
item.description: The move left Lebanon and much of the Mideast in shock as Israeli officials celebrated major military breakthroughs.
item.dc:creator: KAREEM CHEHAYEB and JACK JEFFERY, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Flooding and landslides in Nepal kill at least 66 people, with as many again still missing
item.description: KATHMANDU, Nepal (AP) — Flooding and landslides caused by continuous rainfall has killed at least 66 people in Nepal, and a further 69 are missing, officials said Saturday. Rain has been pouring down since Friday night and is expected to continue over the weekend.
item.dc:creator: BINAJ GURUBACHARYA, Associated Press, WGN 9
